regions.name: G_occipital_middle
regions.function: Processing complex visual shapes and object recognition
regions.designImplication: Complex visual shapes are being processed. Higher activation means more intricate visual elements. Good for rich designs, concerning for cluttered ones.

regions.name: S_oc_sup_and_transversal
regions.function: Connects visual areas for depth and motion processing
regions.designImplication: Depth and motion processing. The brain is analyzing spatial depth cues in the design.

regions.name: S_oc_middle_and_Lunatus
regions.function: Processes visual contours and shape boundaries
regions.designImplication: Visual contour processing. The brain is tracing boundaries and shape edges in the design. Clean lines and clear boundaries activate this.

regions.name: S_oc-temp_med_and_Lingual
regions.function: Connects visual processing to word and face recognition
regions.designImplication: Connecting vision to word and face recognition. This region bridges visual processing with higher-level identification.

regions.name: G_occipital_sup
regions.function: Higher-order visual processing and spatial relationships
regions.designImplication: Higher-order visual processing. Spatial relationships between elements are being analyzed. Higher activation means the layout structure is complex.

regions.name: S_occipital_ant
regions.function: Bridges early vision with higher-level object recognition
regions.designImplication: Bridging early vision with object recognition. The brain is transitioning from seeing shapes to identifying what they are.

regions.name: G_oc-temp_lat-fusifor
regions.function: Processes face recognition and identifying familiar visual objects
regions.designImplication: This design triggers strong face processing. Faces in layouts build trust and human connection. If Image A activates this more, its use of faces or face-like elements is more effective at engaging viewers.

regions.name: S_parieto_occipital
regions.function: Integrates vision with spatial awareness and navigation
regions.designImplication: Integrating vision with spatial navigation. The brain is building a spatial map of the design layout.

regions.name: S_collat_transv_post
regions.function: Processes visual scenes and spatial layout
regions.designImplication: Visual scene processing. The brain is analyzing the overall spatial layout of the design as a complete scene.

regions.name: G_and_S_occipital_inf
regions.function: Early visual processing of objects and faces
regions.designImplication: Early object and face processing. The brain is detecting faces and objects at a pre-conscious level.

regions.name: G_parietal_sup
regions.function: Spatial attention — where you direct your gaze
regions.designImplication: Spatial attention is directed. This region controls where the eye goes. Higher activation means the design has a clear visual hierarchy that guides the viewer's gaze.

regions.name: G_front_inf-Orbital
regions.function: Reward evaluation and emotional response to stimuli
regions.designImplication: This is the reward center. Higher activation means the brain finds this design aesthetically pleasing. This is the strongest predictor of design preference.

regions.name: G_cingul-Post-ventral
regions.function: Emotional memory and autobiographical recall
regions.designImplication: Emotional memory is activated. The design is triggering autobiographical associations. High activation means the design connects to personal emotional memories.

regions.name: G_front_inf-Opercular
regions.function: Language production and interpreting visual meaning
regions.designImplication: Language production and visual meaning. The viewer is interpreting the semantic content of the design.

regions.name: S_intrapariet_and_P_trans
regions.function: Directs spatial attention and tracks where objects are
regions.designImplication: Spatial attention tracking. The viewer's eyes are being directed to specific locations. Higher activation means stronger visual guidance in the design.

regions.name: Lat_Fis-ant-Horizont
regions.function: Separates frontal and temporal language areas
regions.designImplication: Boundary between language areas. Relevant for designs with significant text content.

regions.name: S_orbital_lateral
regions.function: Evaluates reward value and emotional significance
regions.designImplication: Reward evaluation. The brain is assessing the emotional value of what it sees. Higher activation means stronger positive or negative valuation.

regions.name: G_insular_short
regions.function: Interoception and emotional salience — gut reactions
regions.designImplication: Gut reaction center. The insula processes emotional salience. Strong activation means the design provokes a visceral emotional response, positive or negative.

regions.name: G_precuneus
regions.function: Self-referential processing and spatial awareness
regions.designImplication: Self-referential processing. The viewer is connecting the design to their own experience and identity. High activation means the design feels personally relevant.

regions.name: Lat_Fis-ant-Vertical
regions.function: Boundary between motor and language regions
regions.designImplication: Boundary between motor and language regions. Less directly relevant for design evaluation.

composites.label: Reward & Aesthetic Pleasure
composites.interpretation: Similar reward & aesthetic pleasure for both images

composites.label: Cognitive Load
composites.interpretation: Similar cognitive load for both images

composites.label: Visual Processing
composites.interpretation: Image B scores 4% higher. High: Rich visual complexity — clutter if extreme, engaging if moderate. Low: Simple visual input, minimal visual processing.

composites.label: Social & Trust Signals
composites.interpretation: Similar social & trust signals for both images

composites.label: Directed Attention
composites.interpretation: Image B scores 2% higher. High: Strong visual guidance, sustained focused gaze. Low: Unfocused scanning, no clear visual hierarchy.

summary: Image B demonstrates slightly more effective visual engagement and directed attention without increasing cognitive load or reducing aesthetic pleasure compared to Image A.

detailed.winner_reason: Image B demonstrates slightly more effective visual engagement and directed attention without increasing cognitive load or reducing aesthetic pleasure compared to Image A.
detailed.emotional_impact: The brain activation data indicates that neither image elicits a significantly stronger emotional response in terms of aesthetic pleasure or reward. Both designs seem to generate a comparable level of positive or neutral emotional engagement according to the similar 'Reward & Aesthetic Pleasure' signals.
detailed.visual_attention: Image B captures and holds attention slightly better, indicated by a 2% higher 'Directed Attention' signal. Its slightly more complex visual information and processing, reflected in a 4% higher 'Visual Processing' and greater occipital region activation, helps guide the viewer's gaze more effectively.
detailed.memory_retention: Based on the 'Memorability' signal being similar for both, neither image is predicted to be significantly more memorable than the other. Both are equally likely to be recalled.
recommendations: For Image A, enhance the facial features and expression to genuinely activate 'Social & Trust Signals', as the current simple face does not differentiate it from Image B in this regard according to the data.
For Image B, introduce a narrative element or a unique visual style to elevate 'Memorability' and 'Reward & Aesthetic Pleasure', moving beyond just engaging basic visual processing.
For both designs, explore more vibrant or appealing color palettes and refined details to potentially boost 'Reward & Aesthetic Pleasure' and make the content feel less generic and more impactful.
